title: React Fundamentals - Full Course for Beginners
description: This course teaches the fundamentals of React.js by building a simple single-page application that allows users to search and browse popular TV series. It covers core concepts like JSX, components, state management, and routing.

sections.title: Setting React Environment
sections.text_summary: Initial setup of the React development environment.

sections.title: React Elements
sections.text_summary: Understanding React elements and how they differ from HTML.

sections.title: JSX
sections.text_summary: Writing HTML-like syntax within JavaScript using JSX.

sections.title: Components
sections.text_summary: Organizing UI into reusable pieces using components.

sections.title: Props
sections.text_summary: Passing data between components using props.

sections.title: State
sections.text_summary: Managing internal component data with state.

sections.title: Lifecycle (componentDidMount)
sections.text_summary: Handling actions when components mount using lifecycle methods.

sections.title: Asynchronous Calls
sections.text_summary: Making API requests and handling asynchronous data.

sections.title: Containers
sections.text_summary: Difference between components and containers for data management.

sections.title: Lists and Keys
sections.text_summary: Rendering multiple components from a list with unique keys.

sections.title: Refactoring List Component
sections.text_summary: Cleaning up and optimizing list-related code.

sections.title: Forms (Input)
sections.text_summary: Handling user input through React forms.

sections.title: Conditional Rendering
sections.text_summary: Showing different UI based on state or conditions.

sections.title: Images (Loader Component)
sections.text_summary: Creating a loading state for images or data.

sections.title: Setting Routes
sections.text_summary: Defining application paths and their components.

sections.title: Using Route Params
sections.text_summary: Handling dynamic data in URLs using route parameters.
